description: Golden vectors for lineage_id derivation. lineage_id = 'lin:sha256:' + lowercase_hex(SHA-256(utf8(first_version_ctx_id))). The hash input is the UTF-8 bytes of the FULL acdp:// URI string, including the 'acdp://' prefix and the authority. Implementations MUST reproduce these outputs exactly; failure is non-conformance with RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.6. The bundled conformance runner (scripts/conformance-runner.py) executes lin-* fixtures with the same lineage-derivation check it applies to can-* fixtures.

vectors.name: Lineage derivation — primary golden vector
vectors.notes: SHA-256 over the UTF-8 bytes of the full acdp:// URI string. This is the same ctx_id used by sig-001's registry_assigned block and can-001 lineage vector 2 — the three MUST agree.

vectors.name: Lineage derivation — multi-label authority
vectors.notes: Cross-checks can-001 lineage vector 3. A different authority produces a different lineage_id even though the UUID structure is similar — the authority is part of the hash input.

notes: lineage_id derivation also appears inside can-001 (alongside JCS vectors) and is re-checked from sig-001's registry_assigned block. lin-001 is the dedicated, standalone golden-vector fixture for the derivation so the conformance manifest can reference it by a stable id. Registries MUST apply the same formula when walking back through supersedes to the first version (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.6).
